---
title: "squareform"
generated: true
description: "Toggle between vec and square forms of a symmetric matrix."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.linalg.matrix`](../nitrix-linalg-matrix.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:squareform -->
```python
squareform(X: Num[Array, '...']) -> Num[Array, '...']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:squareform -->
Toggle between vec and square forms of a symmetric matrix.

A convenience wrapper: if `X` looks square and symmetric (up to a
rough tolerance), return `sym2vec(X, offset=1)`; otherwise treat it
as a vector and return `vec2sym(X, offset=1)`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:squareform -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `X` | `Num` | `...` | — | Either a square symmetric matrix block to vectorise, or the vectorised strict upper triangle to expand into a matrix. |

**Returns** `Num` — shape `...`. The complementary form: the vectorised strict upper triangle if `X` was a symmetric matrix, otherwise the reconstructed symmetric matrix.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:squareform -->
::: {.callout-note}
Unlike `scipy.spatial.distance.squareform`, this does not verify
that the input is a strictly conforming form.  When in doubt, call
[`sym2vec`](../nitrix-linalg-matrix/sym2vec.qmd) / [`vec2sym`](../nitrix-linalg-matrix/vec2sym.qmd) directly with the explicit
`offset`.
:::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:squareform -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
